description: In memory stores, the value sent to the memory subsystem is exactly the value of the register specified in the store instruction. Properties prove that the rightmost bits of the register are sent to Load Store Unit according to the target address.
example_target: Store with offset 2'h01 (example shown); covers SW, SH, SB at various offsets

description: In memory loads, the value stored in the target register is exactly the value from the memory subsystem.
example_target: Load instructions: LW, LH, LB, LHU, LBU

description: The address sent to the memory subsystem is exactly the effective address given the GPR values and instruction contents (i.e., addressing mode and immediate).
